title: California's 13th Congressional District election, 2026
description: California's 13th Congressional District race for the U.S. House in 2026. The profile reflects currently available public information and may be updated as the race develops.
polling_note: Cook Political Report's CA-13 page is a race summary/rating page rather than a standalone numeric poll, so no confirmed percentages were added.

candidates.name: Adam Gray
candidates.summary: Adam Gray is the U.S. representative for California’s 13th Congressional District and is seeking re-election in 2026. Before returning to Congress, he served in the California State Assembly from 2012 to 2022 and previously worked in state legislative staff roles and as a business owner.

career_history.title: Member
career_history.description: Served for 10 years in the California State Assembly.

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $6.34M in the 2023–2024 cycle, with the largest industry support coming from retired donors ($816,602), Democratic/liberal groups ($673,117), securities & investment interests ($495,898), leadership PACs ($330,767), and lawyers/law firms ($297,573). Top disclosed contributors included AmeriPAC: The Fund for a Greater America ($49,400), Swing Left ($46,075), Digidems PAC ($45,400), Google Inc. ($38,551), and E&J Gallo Winery ($36,300).
candidates.voting_summary: GovTrack shows Adam Gray has served in the U.S. House since Jan. 3, 2025. His profile lists recent key votes on legislation including the Deporting Fraudsters Act of 2026, the Law-Enforcement Innovate to De-Escalate Act, and the Undersea Cable Protection Act of 2025, and it reports that from Jan. 2025 to Mar. 2026 he missed 26 of 470 roll call votes (5.5%).

Healthcare.stance: Gray says he wants to expand access to affordable health care and reduce costs for Valley families. His campaign materials say he does not want people forced to choose between paying for health care and rent, and his House office says he has worked to expand health care access for veterans and rural communities.

sources.title: About — Adam Gray for Congress
Health | Congressman Adam Gray

Economy.stance: Gray’s economic message focuses on lowering food, housing, and utility costs, supporting agriculture and job creation, and reducing red tape for infrastructure projects. His campaign also says he has backed efforts to limit costly tariffs that raise prices for families and farmers.

sources.title: About — Adam Gray for Congress
Issues — Adam Gray for Congress

Climate/Energy.stance: Gray emphasizes water infrastructure, drought resilience, and utility affordability as part of his climate and energy agenda. His campaign and House office highlight investments in water storage, canal repairs, groundwater projects, and permitting reforms for infrastructure projects.

sources.title: Issues — Adam Gray for Congress
Gray Celebrates California Water Infrastructure Investments, Calls for More Resources

Reproductive Rights.stance: Gray is pro-choice and has said he supports a woman’s right to choose and opposes federal abortion restrictions. He has also said abortion decisions should be made by patients and doctors, not politicians.

sources.title: Los Angeles Times: On abortion, Gray and Duarte discuss issues in CA-13
Sacramento Bee: CA-13 candidates on abortion

Immigration.stance: Gray supports comprehensive immigration reform that includes legal status for some undocumented immigrants, border security infrastructure, and changes to visa and asylum systems. He has also said ICE raids are causing fear in the Valley and that there are ways to fix the immigration system without those raids.

sources.title: Congressman Gray Joins Bipartisan Group of Lawmakers to Re-Introduce Comprehensive Immigration Reform Bill
In the District | Congressman Adam Gray

Guns & Safety.stance: No public position found on a specific gun policy in the campaign materials reviewed. His campaign and House office emphasize public safety generally, but they do not publish a detailed gun-control or gun-rights platform on the pages reviewed.

sources.title: Issues — Adam Gray for Congress
Platform — Adam Gray for Congress

Foreign Policy.stance: Gray has said he would not vote to support military action in Iran and favors diplomacy and non-military options first. His office also issued a statement on the Iran war powers resolution.

sources.title: Congressman Gray would not vote to support Iran War, discusses reelection
Gray Statement on Iran War Powers Resolution

Social Justice.stance: Gray has not published a standalone social justice platform on the campaign pages reviewed. His public messaging instead focuses on lowering costs, expanding health care access, improving water and infrastructure, and bipartisan problem-solving.

sources.title: Issues — Adam Gray for Congress
Priorities — Adam Gray for Congress

Education.stance: Gray’s public materials emphasize education as part of his Central Valley agenda, including support for local schools, workforce training, and medical education in the Valley. His House office has highlighted funding for Head Start and Early Head Start programs and his support for expanding medical education opportunities.

sources.title: Education | Congressman Adam Gray
Costa, Gray push to fund new medical schools in the Valley

Tech & AI.stance: No public position found on a standalone Tech & AI policy in the campaign materials reviewed. His office has highlighted agricultural technology and workforce development, but no detailed AI-specific platform was found.

sources.title: Issues — Adam Gray for Congress
Merced College breaks ground on $21 million center geared toward tomorrow’s agriculture jobs

Election Reform.stance: Gray says he supports protecting voting rights and has opposed anti-voter legislation. His office said he voted against a bill he described as anti-voter, and his public materials emphasize fair and accessible elections.

sources.title: Congressman Gray Statement on Voting Against Anti-Voter Bill
Rep. Adam Gray [D-CA13, 2025-2026] - GovTrack

Local Issues.stance: Gray’s local focus is Central Valley water policy. He has backed more water storage, canal repairs, groundwater projects, and streamlined permitting, and his office says he introduced legislation to expand California water infrastructure and protect reliable deliveries for farmers and communities.

sources.title: Rep. Gray Introduces Legislation to Expand California Water Infrastructure
H.R. 6641 - Central Valley Water Solution Act

candidates.name: Daniel Garibay Rodriguez
candidates.summary: Daniel Garibay Rodriguez is a Democratic candidate for U.S. House in California's 13th Congressional District. Public campaign materials and county candidate filings describe him as a Central Valley behavioral health manager focused on due process, mental health and health care access, and better wages and working conditions for working families.

summary_sources.title: Daniel Garibay Rodriguez for Congress | CA-13
Daniel Garibay Rodriguez for Congress | CA-13 Platform

career_history.title: Behavioral Health Manager
career_history.description: Listed on Merced County candidate filings and campaign materials as a behavioral health manager.

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-07. The FEC committee page exists for Daniel Garibay Rodriguez for Congress, but it currently shows no processed receipts or donor totals for the 2025–2026 period.
candidates.voting_summary: Has not held legislative office. Public materials found for his campaign describe him as a Central Valley community leader and behavioral health services manager, but no public legislative voting record or prior elected/executive office was found.

Healthcare.stance: Supports expanding mental health and healthcare access, arguing that care is too expensive and too hard to access in the Central Valley. He says he wants services closer to home and without financial stress or shame.

sources.title: Daniel Garibay Rodriguez for Congress | CA-13 Platform

Economy.stance: Supports equitable wages, strong benefits, and safer working conditions for workers in the Central Valley. His campaign frames these as part of building an economy that allows families to live with dignity.

Immigration.stance: Supports due process and basic rights for all community members regardless of background. His campaign says people should be able to work, go to school, and access health care without fear.

candidates.name: Vin Kruttiventi
candidates.summary: Vin Kruttiventi is a Republican candidate for U.S. House in California's 13th Congressional District. His campaign describes him as a husband, father, community leader, philanthropist, entrepreneur, and startup founder, and says he is focused on small business, education, water, energy, immigration, and public safety.

summary_sources.title: About Vin — Vin Kruttiventi For Congress CA-13
Vin's Priorities — Vin Kruttiventi For Congress CA-13

career_history.title: Entrepreneur and startup founder
career_history.description: Campaign materials and public profiles describe him as an entrepreneur and startup founder.

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $587,845.58 in the 2025–2026 cycle, with a large share coming from candidate self-financing ($333,664.86) and individual contributions ($244,180.72). The FEC summary does not yet show meaningful PAC or industry concentration; the campaign’s disclosed funding is dominated by the candidate’s own money and smaller individual donations.
candidates.voting_summary: Has not held legislative office. Publicly available materials describe him as an entrepreneur and community leader; no public legislative voting record or prior elected/executive office was found. His campaign website emphasizes small business, energy independence, border security, and education priorities.

Economy.stance: He says he would uplift small businesses by reducing regulations, increasing access to capital, and providing growth resources. He also supports lowering taxes on the middle class, cutting wasteful government spending, reducing the national debt, and balancing the budget.

Climate/Energy.stance: He says he supports maintaining energy independence and investing in America-first energy resilience. His campaign materials do not lay out a detailed climate policy beyond that framing.

Immigration.stance: He supports securing the southern border by enforcing immigration laws and creating a realistic, accessible, and legal pathway to citizenship.

sources.title: Vin's Priorities — Vin Kruttiventi For Congress CA-13

Guns & Safety.stance: He says he will fight to expand gun rights and oppose assault-rifle bans, magazine-capacity restrictions, and gun-free zones.

sources.title: Vin's Priorities — Vin Kruttiventi For Congress CA-13

Foreign Policy.stance: He says the United States should protect American interests at home and abroad by maintaining the strongest military in the world. His campaign materials do not provide a more detailed foreign-policy agenda.

Social Justice.stance: He says he rejects DEI-style identity-based policies and favors meritocracy, arguing that opportunities should be earned through ability and effort.

sources.title: I stand with Meritocracy, and reject DEI. #CA13 #Vin4Congress

Tech & AI.stance: He presents himself as a technology entrepreneur and says he founded several startups and runs a global business and technology consulting firm. His campaign materials emphasize job creation and skill-building, but do not provide a detailed AI policy.

Election Reform.stance: He supports congressional term limits and says Congress should be a place of service rather than a career destination.

Local Issues.stance: His local priorities include protecting California water for farmers, supporting small businesses, and promoting energy independence as part of local resilience.

Education.stance: He says every child should have a first-class education regardless of background or zip code.

flags.concern: The summary references specific bills (Deporting Fraudsters Act of 2026, Law-Enforcement Innovate to De-Escalate Act, Undersea Cable Protection Act of 2025) but does not provide voting positions on these bills or links to the individual votes. This makes it difficult to verify the claim independently or understand Gray's actual positions on these matters.
flags.suggestion: Either link to specific votes on GovTrack or clarify whether Gray voted for/against each bill, or remove the specific bill titles and refer generally to his legislative activity.

flags.concern: Daniel Garibay Rodriguez's education array is empty. While the profile correctly documents his lack of prior elected office, his educational background (if public) would be helpful context for a candidate profile.
flags.suggestion: Search public records (LinkedIn, local news, candidate filings, or campaign materials) for educational background; if genuinely unavailable, leave empty but consider noting in the summary that education is not publicly disclosed.

flags.concern: Vin Kruttiventi's education shows 'Not publicly disclosed' across all fields. While it is acceptable to note that information is not available, LinkedIn profile (cited in the links) may contain educational details that should be checked.
flags.suggestion: Review the LinkedIn URL provided in the links (https://www.linkedin.com/in/kruttiventi) to verify whether education is genuinely private or simply not yet retrieved.

flags.concern: Kruttiventi's career_history shows only 'Entrepreneur and startup founder' with no specific company names, dates, or roles. His LinkedIn URL and campaign materials mention 'CEO at A5' and 'global business and technology consulting firm' but the career_history section does not capture these details.
flags.suggestion: Expand career_history with company names (e.g., 'A5'), role titles, and approximate dates from LinkedIn or campaign materials to provide better professional context.

flags.concern: The polling section cites Cook Political Report (2026-04-02) but correctly notes this is a race summary/rating page with no numeric matchup percentages. This is transparent and appropriate, but the entry is minimally informative. Consider whether Cook's current district rating (if available) should be added to context.
flags.suggestion: If available, note Cook's current rating of the district (Likely Democrat, Toss-up, etc.) to give readers a sense of competitiveness.

flags.concern: Adam Gray's Guns & Safety section correctly notes 'low' confidence and states no detailed gun-control or gun-rights platform was found. This is methodologically sound. However, given Gray's voting record in Congress (now in session), GovTrack or his House voting page may contain gun-related votes that should be reviewed.
flags.suggestion: Check gray.house.gov/about/votes-and-legislation or GovTrack for any votes on gun measures to see if an actual position can be inferred from recent legislative activity.

flags.concern: The note that 'no campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-07' with 'no processed receipts or donor totals' is appropriate for an early-stage candidate. However, the FEC committee page (C00942474) should be rechecked closer to primary election (June 2, 2026 per source) to capture updated filings.
flags.suggestion: Flag this for update in May/June 2026 when FEC filings will be more current.

flags.concern: Kruttiventi's self-financing ($333,664.86 of $587,845.58 total, ~57%) is substantial. The summary correctly notes this breakdown, but does not contextualize what this means for his campaign (e.g., whether he is spending down personal wealth or bootstrap-funding). This is accurate reporting but could benefit from context.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a note in the narrative (or summary) that his campaign is heavily self-funded, which may affect his grassroots fundraising dynamics.

reviews.summary: This is a well-researched, balanced profile of the CA-13 House race with strong sourcing across all three candidates. The profile demonstrates thorough effort to document policy positions from official campaign sites, government sources (Congress.gov, FEC, GovTrack), and credible news outlets. Candidate backgrounds are accurately documented with proper source citations. The language is neutral and nonpartisan throughout. Gaps in issue coverage (e.g., no Healthcare position for Kruttiventi, no Climate position for Garibay Rodriguez) are appropriately flagged as "no public position found" after documented search efforts rather than left blank, which is methodologically sound. The polling section transparently notes why percentages are absent. Minor suggestions for enhancement noted below, but no factual errors or bias detected.

flags.concern: The profile identifies Adam Gray as the incumbent for the 119th Congress (starting 2025). My training data indicates this district was historically held by John Duarte (R) as of the 118th Congress. However, the profile provides specific, current source URLs (GovTrack, house.gov, and BioGuide) and legislative dates for Gray starting in Jan 2025.
flags.suggestion: Human reviewer should confirm the results of the 2024 CA-13 election to ensure incumbency status is current for the 2026 cycle.

flags.concern: Several issues for Daniel Garibay Rodriguez (Climate, Reproductive Rights, Guns, Education, Tech, Election Reform, Foreign Policy) are marked as 'No public position found.'
flags.suggestion: This is likely due to the candidate's status as a first-time or less-prominent challenger. Reviewer may check for updated social media or local forum entries as the 2026 primary approaches.

flags.concern: Mentions specific 2025 and 2026 legislation (e.g., 'Deporting Fraudsters Act of 2026') which cannot be verified against current real-world historical records as of late 2024.
flags.suggestion: Treat as accurate within the 2026 research context provided by the agent, but confirm bill titles against official Congress.gov records once in the 2026 calendar year.

reviews.summary: This is an exceptionally high-quality research profile. It demonstrates excellent internal consistency and proactive research, particularly in documenting the legislative record of the incumbent (Adam Gray) for the 2025-2026 session. The sourcing is diverse, including official .gov resources, campaign sites, and independent financial tracking from the FEC and OpenSecrets. The profile handles a multi-candidate field with neutrality, clearly distinguishing between the incumbent's voting record and the challengers' platform-based stances.

This profile is a model of thorough and reliable research, with strong sourcing from official government databases, campaign websites, and credible news outlets that directly support claims across all candidates and issues. It excels in documenting absences of public positions for less prominent candidates without fabricating details, maintains impeccable neutrality in language, and provides detailed, internally consistent backgrounds that align with cited sources, making it an excellent resource for understanding the CA-13 race.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 93/100.
